Insignia Protector  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: SECONDARY
AC: 20
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
CHA: +5 WIS: +10 INT: +10 MANA: +20
SV FIRE: +10 SV COLD: +10 SV MAGIC: +10
WT: 3.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)
Insignia Protector
Lore Item Placeable
Slot: SECONDARY
AC: 20
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
CHA: +5 WIS: +10 INT: +10 MANA: +20
SV FIRE: +10 SV COLD: +10 SV MAGIC: +10
WT: 3.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Type:Shield
Light:Stein of Moggok
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:3 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:3480
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 04:16:11
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Average Price: 20,000pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Ultra Rare
Level to Attain: 55
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Chardok
NPC Name
Queen Velazul Di`zok


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Chardok
Has the same appearance as the Sarnak Battle Shield.

stats

Uploaded August 12th, 2019 by stickleback

Bought one as well
QuoteReply
# Aug 12 2004 at 8:16 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591799304856
2 posts
I just bought one...on, of all servers, Strommm the very overpriced newbie server...for 7500pp. Had no idea it was ultra rare, just liked the stats for twinking my enchanter, no recommended level and Cha...better in this case than a Bulwark. Oh and by the way...graphic is the win, gold snake down the middle - Rocks! Anyone know if this thing is dropping again?

Saihra 65 Cleric
Kashra 52 Mage
Zaihra 40 Enchanter

i have one
QuoteReply
# Mar 14 2003 at 1:08 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591786507094
2 posts
went on a raid for the cleric epic piece
we took down the queen too and the shield droped
yaaaaa i won

i love it
most people dont believe im a wiz because wizards cant use the battle shield and thats what everyone thinks it is.
i also wear enchanted chain and i carry a knotted wooden mallet hehe so i look like a SK

good luck to you all that want one - since i got it i have been keeping my eye out for anyone else with it and i have not seen a single one


twedlit
52 wizzy
sol ro
syn ro

came back in to post taht i got it last sunday so YES it still drops ( read a post asking if it drops anymore )

Well,the Prexus server just had one drop for the Bonds Of Faith and is carried by Revell Mezzalot-Enchanter extreme!! It is said it is only the second on this server, but to answer your first question, obviously yes it drops, and wow, what a drop it is!!


-Larrs-
▲ Page top
interrupts
QuoteReply
# Oct 12 2001 at 4:52 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591768835427
15 posts
I am a Mage pure caster lvl 51, and i regain concentration probaly 2/3 of the times i get hit during casting. I believe that to be a tolerable ratio. More often than not. My wis is only 80
▲ Page top
RE: interrupts
QuoteReply
# Sep 29 2002 at 11:28 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592252422330
123 posts
Interupts seem to depend a lot on your movement.(ie loc)If you move from being hit you massively increase your chance of being interupted.
The reason you sometimes fail if you cast too soon after moving is that the server has not been told you stopped before casting(assumption).Hence the change direction/turn trick before casting gives a loc update to server.Anyway this leads me to suspect it is as much the change in loc as the actual getting hit that affects recovery.If dex/ac helps you avoid getting hit and avoid being moved by said hit(because it missed) then indeed it does affect interrupts.Although I would never dispute that channelling ability is critical to channel through hits that land.I just think dex/ac helps you avoid a skill check against channelling to start with.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 10 2001 at 3:13 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I am on the Rathe server and if anyone wants to trade or sell one I am offering good trades and off course money talks. Give me a tell at the name below.
#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 10 2001 at 2:50 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Int Casters can use wisdom in one positive way - Wisdom is the stat that determines how often you continue casting after an interruption. Most pure casters never see "you regain concentration and continue casting" but ask your local cleric how often they see it with their maxed out wisdom.
RE: Int casters and Wisdom
QuoteReply
# Sep 19 2001 at 12:59 PM Rating: Excellent
Uggliey
15 posts
Totally wrong!! The ONLY thing that effects recovery of casting is the Channeling skill. Verant confirmed this before when they stated how differant stats worked and what they did. The ONLY thing wisdom effects is mana for wis casters.

I believe (not totally sure) that if your wisdom is higher than INT all your trade skills will use WIS to gain skill and for success rolls. If your INT is higher it will use that stat.
____________________________
Uggliey, Shadow Knight
Of the Kithicor Server, EQ2
▲ Page top
RE: Int casters and Wisdom
QuoteReply
# Nov 05 2001 at 5:18 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361623581
View User Forum
9 posts
Also, most clerics that can channel casts through being hit also have ac rivaling that of the warriors they are healing, makes those hits much easier to handle. Besides, my wizard channels through and regains concentration more times than I want to admit with things like root and final killing nukes, his wis though is bottomed out in the gutter :p
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 04 2001 at 11:32 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I have one of these on my Necro nice item took 3 days of camping in a full group to get it though good luck to the rest of you trying to get it =)
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 04 2001 at 11:31 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post)

# Jun 18 2001 at 5:53 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
See if you can kill her first. I guarantee that unless you are in a very powerful guild you won't even see the queen hit the floor to loot her to then roll for this shield. And seeing as how it's her rarest drop... sorry guys. Dream on.
▲ Page top
Wisdom?
QuoteReply
# Jun 13 2001 at 11:46 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760741916
4 posts
Its a nice item and all but what is the point in it having +10 Wisdom?

The only classes that can use it are Enchanter, Wizard, Magician and Necromancer, all 4 of these classes dont use wisdom...

Other than that, sweet item.
▲ Page top
RE: Wisdom?
QuoteReply
# Sep 29 2002 at 11:31 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592252422330
123 posts
I once read that wisdom helps resist fear and increases the chance of breaking fear earlier.I have no proof of this.
▲ Page top
RE: Wisdom?
QuoteReply
# Aug 01 2001 at 11:37 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
This is Verant at its best -- providing worthless stats to every class. The Chardok queen is not THAT hard -- perhaps you've heard of a little thing called STRATEGY? It means you go in there with a plan, not just nuking and hacking away like morons. You know who you are -- you've been walking indiscriminately into my camps and KSing me since my first character was about level 10. Obviously the Queen can be killed --but Verant isn't going to make it easy. I agree the warrior epic is not the best weapon in the game, but there is a certain amount of prestige involved. For those of us to which this game means something, it represents the completion of a long and involved quest, complete with dragon and plane raids. Just my opinion, but the quest for "uber loot" is much less meaningful to me than being able to say I completed my epic with the help of my guild.
▲ Page top
RE: Wisdom?
QuoteReply
# Aug 13 2001 at 11:39 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591754605784
2 posts
you are all missing a very important stat...
The AC. some of us Chanters with maxed out cha and int, are looking to advance our AC. and 20 is a very nice number IMHO...
▲ Page top
RE: Wisdom?
QuoteReply
# Oct 12 2001 at 4:16 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591758961202
60 posts
My Warrior has maxxed charisma. Everyone loves him. He cant tank at all but all the mobs love him.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 19 2001 at 1:40 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Enchanter's DO use wisdom, silly.
RE: Wisdom?
QuoteReply
# Sep 10 2002 at 4:06 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591777601987
71 posts
As stated by VI, success rate only depends on your skill level in the skill. The greater of Int or Wis in this case only affects your chances of increasing that skill.

The same is true with Channeling too, Dex affected the chance of raising the skill but did not affect the success of the skill at all.
▲ Page top
RE: Wisdom?
QuoteReply
# Jul 09 2001 at 8:53 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763456502
5 posts
And jewelry (or any other trade skill for that matter) uses whichever is higher. So there really is no point unless somehow their wis is going to surpass their int with this item.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 28 2001 at 6:42 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Wis sucses rate int skill increase
RE: Wisdom?
QuoteReply
# Aug 21 2001 at 8:38 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250403107
10 posts
Nope you are wrong. As Wrexen stated tradeskills use whichever is higher, int or wis.
Lots of people still believe that wis affects succes rate and int leveling rate, but that was a misunderstood.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 15 2001 at 1:45 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post)
price
QuoteReply
# Jan 07 2001 at 6:30 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
how much does anyone think this would sell for? 20ac, 5cha, 10int, 20 mana and 10 to 3 saves. it is caster only however. well, i would imagine more then 20k as a GBS will sell for 15-20k and thats 9int and 10 save vs magic, whereas this has 1 more int, 2 more +10 saves, and 20 more ac, and looks like the sarnak battle shield on an int caster. wow.
